Of course maloo was asked to only run ost-pools so it id not catch it.

Patch Set 1: -Code-Review

I have a more comprehensive version of this fix, since there are many places that assume < 10 OSTs or MDTs.  I haven't had the time to review the test failures, but it would be great if someone could refresh that patch to fix all of these cases, rather than just this one case.

Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

(1 comment)

Patch Set 1:

I've refreshed patch https://review.whamcloud.com/38058 "LU-12506 tests: clean up MDT name generation" so it may be that it also fixes the issues here.  There is no real reason that the pool code has to maintain both decimal and hex index values now, which simplifies the code.

It would be great if you could test whether that patch fixes your issue (there is no real test case here, since the Test-Parameters: line didn't specify more than 10 OSTs).
Uploaded patch set 2.

commit.subject: LU-13620 tests: pool_add_targets() fix
commit.message:
LU-13620 tests: pool_add_targets() fix

Fix pool_add_targets to don't fail if number of
OSTs is >= 10 - lctl expects them in a hex view.
Check result of "lctl pool_add". Pass only if
the result is either 0 or 17(EEXIST).
Finally, make pool_add_targets to check that were
added only requested OSTs, i.e. don't fail to add
OST0 if OST1 is already in a pool.

Test-Parameters: trivial testlist=ost-pools
HPE-bug-id: LUS-8723
HPE-bug-id: LUS-8941
Change-Id: I841b3db3a89dbc86075cd23b7d71764ffb849181
Signed-off-by: Elena Gryaznova <elena.gryaznova@hpe.com>
Signed-off-by: Sergey Cheremencev <sergey.cheremencev@hpe.com>
Reviewed-on: https://review.whamcloud.com/38803
Tested-by: jenkins <devops@whamcloud.com>
Tested-by: Maloo <maloo@whamcloud.com>
Reviewed-by: Andreas Dilger <adilger@whamcloud.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>
